conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 06:30

corpname.part: Yale Peabody Museum of Natural History

did.abstract: The archives of Wendell Clark Bennett, accumulated through his time at Yale.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Wendell Clark Bennett Archives, Anthropology, Yale Peabody Museum

bioghist.head: Biographical Sketch
bioghist.p: Wendell Bennett (b.1905, d.1953) was a research associate in anthropology from 1945 to 1953. Bennett's major contribution to the anthropology collections were in South American archaeology, particularly the Huari material from Peru and other collections from Columbia and Ecuador that are curated by the Museum.
- Research staff at Bishop Museum, Hawaii (1928-1929)
- Associate Professor of Anthropology at the University of Wisconsin (1938-1940)
- Professor of Anthropology at Yale University

scopecontent.head: Description of the Material
scopecontent.p: The archives of Wendell Bennet include correspondence, research material and photgraphs from North and South America as well as lecture notes and lantern slides.

did.unittitle: Linguistic Maps. Linguistic Maps of South America.

Bolivia. Part 1 of 2: Cochabamba, Arani, Colcapirhua, Iluri, Cayacayani, Coati Island, Titicaca Island, Southeast and South Shore Lake Titicaca, Sillustani, Tiahuanaco, Chiripa, Paco, Quehuava, Pariti, Cascachi Temple, Lucurmata, Intja, Yayescpuente, Copacabana, Chocupercas, Riberalta.

Bolivia. Part 2 of 2: Cochabamba, Arani, Colcapirhua, Iluri, Cayacayani, Coati Island, Titicaca Island, Southeast and South Shore Lake Titicaca, Sillustani, Tiahuanaco, Chiripa, Paco, Quehuava, Pariti, Cascachi Temple, Lucurmata, Intja, Yayescpuente, Copacabana, Chocupercas, Riberalta.
Colombia. Part 1 of 2: San Agustin, Tierra dentro, Putumayo, Narino, Upper Cauca, Quimbaya, Sinu, Chibcha, Santa Marta, Tairona, Baranquilla.
Colombia. Part 2 of 2: San Agustin, Tierra dentro, Putumayo, Narino, Upper Cauca, Quimbaya, Sinu, Chibcha, Santa Marta, Tairona, Baranquilla.
Ecuador. Cuenca, Inga Pircha, Ibarra, East Ecuador, Jivaros, Rio Napo.

Peru. Bennett Photographs (Part 1 of 3): Bronze, Gold, Textiles, Indians, Corn, South Coast, Nazca, Pacheco, Palpa, Ica, Chincha, Paracas, Mummy, Pachacamac, Para-Monga, Huarmey, Viru, Pucara, Cuzvo, Saccahuaman, Ollantaitambo, Machu Picchu, Lambayeque, Tucume, Gayoso, Chongoyape, Casma, Nepena, Chao, Chan Chan, Trujillo, Moche, Las Delicias, Chicama, El Brujo, Pata de Burro, Pucuche, Salmanca, Playa, Chavin, Mochica, Late Chimu.

Peru. Bennett Photographs (Part 2 of 3): Bronze, Gold, Textiles, Indians, Corn, South Coast, Nazca, Pacheco, Palpa, Ica, Chincha, Paracas, Mummy, Pachacamac, Para-Monga, Huarmey, Viru, Pucara, Cuzvo, Saccahuaman, Ollantaitambo, Machu Picchu, Lambayeque, Tucume, Gayoso, Chongoyape, Casma, Nepena, Chao, Chan Chan, Trujillo, Moche, Las Delicias, Chicama, El Brujo, Pata de Burro, Pucuche, Salmanca, Playa, Chavin, Mochica, Late Chimu.

Peru. Bennett Photographs (Part 3 of 3): Bronze, Gold, Textiles, Indians, Corn, South Coast, Nazca, Pacheco, Palpa, Ica, Chincha, Paracas, Mummy, Pachacamac, Para-Monga, Huarmey, Viru, Pucara, Cuzvo, Saccahuaman, Ollantaitambo, Machu Picchu, Lambayeque, Tucume, Gayoso, Chongoyape, Casma, Nepena, Chao, Chan Chan, Trujillo, Moche, Las Delicias, Chicama, El Brujo, Pata de Burro, Pucuche, Salmanca, Playa, Chavin, Mochica, Late Chimu.
Bennett Manuscript Plates and Figures. Box 1 of 3 boxes.
Bennett Manuscript Plates and Figures. Box 2 of 3 boxes.
Bennett Manuscript Plates and Figures. Box 3 of 3 boxes.
Hawaii Manuscript. Hawaii Manuscript and notes.
Andes Ethnology. Split over two archive boxes.
Chile. Research notes on the Atacamenta.
Hopi. Research notes on the Hopi.
Iriquois. Research notes on the Iriquois.

Miscellaneous. Tarahumara MS; Middle America-Costa Rica; Mexico (Simpson); Columbia Diary, 1941; Chibcha; Santa Marta; Quimbaya; Tierra Aduetio; San Augustine; Narino; Ecuador Cuenca; Colombia, Preuss, K.J, 1929; Nambiquara; Aymara Texts; Bolivian Labor; Aymara; Middle America; Tarahumara-Linguistics; Bennett, et al. Argentine; Tarahumara.
Oneida. Research notes on the Oneida.
Miscellaneous. Miscellaneous bibliographies, some duplicates, 14 folders.
South American Ethnology. 4 of 5 boxes of South American Ethnology Bibliography. Boxes 1, 2, 3 and 4.
South American Ethnology. 1 of 5 boxes of South American Ethnology Bibliography. Box 5.
North American Ethnographic. MacKenzie, Northwest Coast, Plains, Southeast, Eastern Woodlands, Plateau.
Exchange of Students and Research Personell. 4 folders.
Illustrations. Pottery Illustrations, Notes and Plates.
Joint Committe on Latin American Studies. 4 binders, notes removed from binders.
Miscellaneous. Glass slides, film rolls, etc.
Miscellaneous. Film roll, Drawing of La Mata Mound Section, Venezuela, and pottery samples.
Miscellaneous. Duplicate papers/reports; Conference of Bolivian Indians; Kirchoff, P. 1931; Roop, W.F. Watercraft in Amazonia; Peru Metals.
Newspaper Clippings. Miscellaneous folder of newspaper clippings.
Personnel File. North and South America.
Reports to the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs. Miscellaneous Reports to the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs.
Social Science Research Council. Notes on the Social Science Research Council, 21 folders.
Lantern Slides. Maps, Scenes, Coast Periods, Casma, Recuay-Charin.
